pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) specifies this exact filename; setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both reference this name
Task description (line 5) explicitly states 'generate a PNG image named tactical_map.png'; confirmed in setup_task.sh line 5 and export_result.sh line 6
setup_task.sh line 15 creates this directory; line 18-23 creates environment.ini; line 25-32 creates ownship.ini; line 34-48 creates othership.ini with 2 vessels
setup_task.sh line 51 creates this directory; line 54-72 creates both ownship.ini (1 ship) and othership.ini (2 vessels) with hardcoded coordinates

setup_task.sh creates hidden scenario with: 1 ownship (Secret Ownship) + 2 otherships (Secret Traffic 1 at 0.01,0.01 and Secret Traffic 2 at -0.01,-0.01) = 3 total vessels. Verified by examining lines 54-72 of setup_task.sh and verifier.py comment on line 91 confirming '3 ships total'

privileged_info_summary: The task requires an agent to create a Python script 'generate_tactical_map.py' that parses Bridge Command simulation INI files and generates tactical maps using matplotlib. The test scenario (Portsmouth Approach Custom) contains 3 total vessels: 1 own ship (MV Target at Lat 50.79, Long -1.11) and 2 traffic vessels (Incoming Tanker and Crossing Ferry). The hidden validation scenario also contains exactly 3 vessels: 1 own ship at (0.0, 0.0) and 2 traffic vessels at (0.01, 0.01) and (-0.01, -0.01). All vessel data is synthetic and created by the setup script with hardcoded coordinates. The expected output is a PNG file named 'tactical_map.png' containing a plot of vessel positions with text labels and directional arrows.
